calendar.matters:
CRC 3.1385 ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Jul-19-2022 Dismissal
MINI MINUTES: No appearance. Notice having been given and there being no appearance or objection filed, the Court orders the balance of the case dismissed pursuant to California Rules of Court Section 3.1385. Judge: ANNE-CHRISTINE MASSULLO; Clerk: GGonzales; Proceedings not reported.

CRC 3.1385 ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar May-02-2022 Continued To Jul-19-22 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Motion To Enter Stipulated Judgment
Off Calendar Mar-29-2022 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF PARTNERS PERSONNEL-MANAGEMENT SERVICES, LLC's Motion To Enter Stipulated Judgment.

Plaintiff's motion for entry of judgment pursuant to stipulation is granted in part. Plaintiff is entitled to a judgment for $48,000 plus interest.

While plaintiff contended that it was owed $161,834.43 for defendant's breach of the services agreement, the parties agreed to settle the case for $100,000. Defendant has paid $52,000 of the settlement amount but then stopped making payments. Plaintiff cannot seek the remainder of the $161,834.43 because that would constitute an illegal penalty. The amount that plaintiff seeks bears no reasonable relationship to the range of damages the parties could have anticipated would result from defendant's failure timely to pay the settlement amount. (See Vitatech Internat., Inc. v. Sporn (2017) 16 Cal.App.5th 796, 801; Greentree Financial Group, Inc. v. Execute Sports, Inc. (2008) 163 Cal.App.4th 495.) Plaintiff is therefore entitled to a judgment for $48,000 plus interest.

Counsel for plaintiff is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Courts ruling and proposed judgment.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer, Clerk: W. Trupek, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano CSR# 8600, email: maria.torreano@gmail.com mailto:maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. (302/RBU)
